subject: DevOps
title: Test Your Assumptions
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Test Your Assumptions.

hook.text: GitLab assumed backups worked. BA assumed DR would failover. Assumptions kill reliability.

buildup.text: We assume backups run, failover works, and rollback is fast. But do we verify?

discovery.text: Restore from backup regularly. Fail over to DR periodically. Test rollback before you need it.

twist.text: Game days and chaos engineering exist to test assumptions. Break things before they break you.

climax.text: Write down your critical assumptions. Then test each one. Schedule it. Track the results.

punchline.text: An untested assumption is a risk in disguise.

quiz.question: What is the best way to validate operational assumptions?
options: Test them regularly through drills and chaos engineering
Trust that they work based on the initial setup
Wait until a real incident reveals the truth
